Clinical_Effect: NSAIDs may reduce the antihypertensive effect of ACE inhibitors and increase the risk of renal impairment
Management: Monitor blood pressure and renal function; use the lowest effective NSAID dose

Clinical_Effect: Increased risk of gastrointestinal bleeding and ulceration
Management: Avoid routine combination; if unavoidable consider gastroprotection and monitor for bleeding

Clinical_Effect: NSAIDs may reduce ACE inhibitor efficacy and increase the risk of acute kidney injury
Management: Monitor renal function blood pressure and hydration; avoid prolonged combined use

Clinical_Effect: Dual renin-angiotensin system blockade increases the risk of hypotension hyperkalemia and renal impairment
Management: Avoid dual RAS blockade unless specifically indicated; monitor renal function and potassium closely

Clinical_Effect: NSAIDs may reduce antihypertensive effect and increase renal risk
Management: Monitor blood pressure and kidney function

Clinical_Effect: Amlodipine may increase atorvastatin exposure leading to higher myopathy risk
Management: Use recommended atorvastatin doses and monitor for muscle symptoms

Clinical_Effect: May increase dizziness somnolence and fall risk
Management: Monitor CNS effects especially in elderly patients
Combination is generally appropriate in patients at GI risk

Clinical_Effect: Usually no clinically significant interaction at therapeutic doses
Management: Use as prescribed and monitor total analgesic use

Clinical_Effect: No clinically significant interaction is generally expected
